skill.name: code-review-playbook
skill.description: Provides a structured playbook for code reviews, including review process guidance, conventional comment formats, checklists, pull request templates, and feedback templates.
skill.summary: Standardize code reviews with practical checklists, comment patterns, and reusable templates.
skill.author: AI Agent Hub

security_audit.summary: Static command, crypto, reconnaissance, and network flags were reviewed as Markdown examples, checklist labels, or documentation links rather than executable behavior. No malicious intent, prompt-injection text, credential exfiltration, or runnable command execution was found. The main concern is that copyable LLM review automation examples interpolate untrusted diffs and code into prompts without prompt-injection guidance.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Code Interpolated Into LLM Review Prompts
medium_findings.description: The skill includes copyable TypeScript examples that place PR diffs, codebase context, and source code directly inside LLM prompt strings. This is not executable by the skill itself, but users who adopt the pattern should add prompt-injection boundaries and treat reviewed code as untrusted input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines show direct interpolation of PR diffs and code into prompt content. The risk is moderate because the content is instructional Markdown, not code executed by the skill.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Flags Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections come from Markdown fences, inline code, and review instructions such as git checkout, npm audit, pip-audit, and TypeScript examples. No script file, runtime hook, or automatic command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged locations are clearly Markdown examples or checklist text. The repository contains only Markdown files and no executable script entry point.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto and Reconnaissance Flags Are Review Vocabulary
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance detections are checklist and template language for reviewers. They describe what users should look for in reviewed code and do not implement crypto, scanning, or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are checklist items or data structures for reporting review findings. I found no code that performs cryptographic operations or reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: The network flags point to an Anthropic documentation link and a localhost testing placeholder. They do not send data to an external service and do not indicate exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Anthropic URL is a documentation citation and the localhost URL is a reviewer testing placeholder. Neither location contains a network request or secret-bearing payload.

content.value_statement: Inconsistent reviews slow teams and make feedback harder to act on. This skill provides checklists, comment formats, and templates for clear review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Provides a step-by-step code review process for reviewers and authors.
Defines conventional comment labels for severity and intent.
Includes broad checklists for quality, testing, security, APIs, databases, and deployment.
Supplies pull request and review feedback templates for consistent documentation.
Gives examples for constructive feedback, review decisions, and follow-up actions.
limitations: Does not execute tests, linters, or security scanners by itself.
Does not replace human judgment for architecture or product tradeoffs.
Examples may need adaptation to team-specific tools and review policies.
LLM automation snippets require additional prompt-injection safeguards before production use.

use_cases.title: Review Pull Requests Consistently
use_cases.description: Use the checklist and comment labels to review changes with clear severity and actionable feedback.

use_cases.title: Prepare Changes Before Review
use_cases.description: Run a self-review with the pull request template before asking teammates for feedback.

use_cases.title: Define Team Review Standards
use_cases.description: Adopt the templates and process guidance as a baseline for team review norms.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Small Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review-playbook to review this small pull request. Focus on correctness, tests, and clear conventional comments.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a PR Description
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the pull request template from the code-review-playbook to help me prepare a complete PR description and testing notes.

prompt_templates.title: Triage Review Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the review feedback patterns to classify these comments as blocking, non-blocking, questions, or nitpicks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Team Review Standard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review-playbook to create a team review policy with severity labels, reviewer expectations, and merge criteria.

output_examples.input: Review a PR that adds payment retry logic and new tests.
output: Summary of the change and review decision.
Blocking issues grouped by severity and file location.

output_examples.input: Help write a PR description for a new API endpoint.
output: Concise description of what changed and why.
Security, database, and compatibility checklist items.

output_examples.input: Turn informal review notes into conventional comments.
output: Comments labeled as issue, suggestion, question, nitpick, praise, or security.
Blocking status is made explicit where needed.
Each comment explains impact and recommended action.

best_practices: Review the pull request description and tests before reading line-by-line changes.
Use conventional labels to make severity and merge impact clear.
Separate must-fix defects from preferences and follow-up improvements.
anti_patterns: Blocking a merge for subjective style preferences that automation can handle.
Leaving vague feedback without explaining the impact or a possible fix.
Approving large or risky changes without checking tests, security, and rollout impact.

faq.question: Does this skill run code review tools automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides review guidance, checklists, and templates. You must run tests and scanners separately.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What review style does it encourage?
faq.answer: It encourages specific, kind, actionable feedback with clear severity labels.

faq.question: Is it only for security reviews?
faq.answer: No. It covers quality, testing, architecture, performance, documentation, and security checks.

faq.question: Can teams customize the templates?
faq.answer: Yes. Teams should adapt labels, merge rules, and checklist items to their codebase.

faq.question: What is the main security caution?
faq.answer: Copyable LLM review examples should add prompt-injection safeguards before production use.
